semanticCore: Detachments, separate groups, military divisions, or gathered companies

keyDerivations.meaning: Detachment of soldiers, company, group of horsemen, or distinct assembly

figurativeOrRareUsages: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا خُذُوا حِذْرَكُمْ فَانْفِرُوا ثُبَاتٍ أَوِ انْفِرُوا جَمِيعًا (4:71): O you who have believed, take your precaution, and go forth in separate groups/detachments, or go forth all together.
discardedNoise: Yathrib (old name of Medina) - spelling confusion.
summaryEn: The root refers to military units or separate companies (Thubah), giving strategic marching advice to the early Muslims to deploy either in smaller scout groups or as a combined army.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر الجماعة من الناس وعصائب المقاتلين وخيول الغزاة المتفرقة (ثبي)، مصوراً خروج المسلمين للجهاد إما في سرايا متفرقة (ثبات) أو جيش واحد.
rawText: ثبى , (K and TA, in the CK تَثَيْتَلَ,) He feigned himself stupid after feigning himself intelligent: (K, TA:) accord. to some copies, after feigning himself negligent, or inadvertent: (تَغَافُل being put in the place of تَعَاقُل:) [app. from the subst. below:] but the word as mentioned by IAar is تنتل [app. a mistranscription for ثَنْتَلَ]. (TA.)
